ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date and time the customer registered */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "middle_name" TEXT /* Middle name of the customer */,
  "email_address" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "home_phone" TEXT /* Home phone number of the customer */,
  "mobile_phone" TEXT /* Mobile phone number of the customer */,
  "work_phone" TEXT /* Work phone number of the customer */,
  "street" TEXT /* Street address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the customer resides */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the customer resides */,
  "zipcode" INTEGER /* Zipcode of the customer's address */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language of the customer */,
  "marketing_opt_in" INTEGER /* Whether the customer has opted in to receive marketing communications */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last updated date and time of the customer's record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "registration_channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each registration channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the registration channel (e.g., in-person, phone, online) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the registration channel */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the registration channel (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the registration channel was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the registration channel was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_channels" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the registration channel used by the customer */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date the customer registered through the channel */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the customer's registration through the channel */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the registration (e.g., advertisement, referral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id", "channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, sales representative, customer support agent) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the customer data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the system */,
  "action" TEXT /* Specific action performed during the access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
